quarantine.note: Tremont Avenue evidence mixes full corridor identity with shorter busway subsegments; do not promote until full-corridor vs subsegment scope is resolved.

original_rationale: Both records describe Tremont Avenue in the Bronx with matching primary limits (Sedgwick Avenue to Boston Road) from authoritative sources: corridor_tremont-ave from the 2019 Final Plan page 32, and corridor_tremont-avenue from the 2021 Addendum table (p005_c0003) which explicitly lists Tremont Avenue with from=Sedgwick Avenue, to=Boston Road. Routes overlap substantially (Bx18, Bx36, Bx40, Bx42 common; Bx4A and Bx9 in the 2019 plan only). The records share identity key 'corridor_tremont-ave' and the validator flagged duplicate_global_identity. The conflicting limits field 'University Avenue to Bronx River Parkway' in record 2 appears to be a broader/superset description from a different observation (better_buses), but the core from/to fields and the addendum evidence confirm the same Sedgwick-to-Boston segment. The busway sub-segments in record 2's evidence (Third Avenue to Southern Boulevard) describe a shorter project within this corridor. Confidence is medium because of the conflicting limits label, but the structural evidence strongly supports a single canonical corridor.
required_follow_up: Resolve blocker before creating accepted merge/do-not-merge overrides.
Do not apply this packet directly to data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
